sales.title:
EMPIRE BYZANTIN - BYZANTINE
Justin II (565-578). Solidus ND, Constantinople, 2e officine. NGC MS 5/5 5/5 (3990670-008).
Av. D N I - VSTI - NVS P P AVI. Buste de Justin II, casqué, diadémé et cuirassé de face, tenant de la main droite un globe nicéphore et de la main gauche un bouclier orné d'un cavalier galopant à droite.
Rv. VICTORI - A AVGGG (officine) / CONOB. Constantinople assise de face, la tête tournée à droite, tenant un sceptre long de la main droite et un globe crucigère de la main gauche.
BC.345 - Sommer 5.2 ; Or - 4,47 g - 21 mm - 6 h
NGC MS 5/5 5/5 (3990670-008).
De frappe un peu faible. Quasiment dans son état de frappe. Superbe à Fleur de coin.

sales.description:
Ancients
Justin II (AD 565-578). AV solidus (20mm, 4.55 gm, 5h). NGC MS 4/5 - 4/5, edge cut. Constantinople, 4th officina, AD 565-578. D N I-VSTI-NVS PP AVI, pearl-diademed, helmeted, and cuirassed bust of Justin II facing, with beard, wearing pendilia, Victory standing right on globe in right hand, crowning Justin II, shield decorated with horseman motif in left / VICTORI-A AVGGG Δ, Constantinopolis seated facing, helmeted head right, grounded scepter in right hand, globus cruciger in outstre...

sales.description:
JUSTIN II, 565-578. AV Solidus (4.44 gms), Constantinople Mint, 8th Officina, 567-578. NGC Ch AU, Strike: 3/5 Surface: 3/5. Adjusted Flan.
To view all items from the John P. Burnham Collection, click  here.
S-345. Obverse: Helmeted and cuirassed bust facing, holding shield and globus surmounted by crowning Victory; Reverse: Constantinopolis seated facing on throne, head right, holding spear and globus cruciger; H//CONOB.

From the John P. Burnham Collection.

Estimate: 400 - 600...
